task_completion.description: Create a new forensic case with the specified name and case number.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Autopsy 'New Case Information' dialog showing Case Name 'Court_Prep_2024' and Case Number 'INV-TAG-006', or the main Autopsy window displaying this case name in the title bar.

task_completion.description: Add the specified disk image as a data source to the case.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Autopsy 'Add Data Source' wizard showing the path '/home/ga/evidence/ntfs_undel.dd' being selected or processed.

task_completion.description: Process the image using only the File Type Identification module.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Autopsy 'Configure Ingest Modules' window showing ONLY the 'File Type Identification' checkbox selected, with all other modules deselected.

task_completion.description: Tag deleted files (including those with a red X or in $OrphanFiles) as 'Notable Item' with the required comment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Autopsy UI showing deleted files selected, and a 'Create Tag' or 'Tag File' dialog applying the 'Notable Item' tag with the comment 'Deleted file - evidence of potential data destruction'.

task_completion.description: Tag allocated regular files as 'Follow Up' with the required comment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Autopsy UI showing regular allocated files selected, and a 'Create Tag' or 'Tag File' dialog applying the 'Follow Up' tag with the comment 'Active file - content review required for relevance'.

task_completion.description: Generate a formal HTML report for the case.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Autopsy 'Generate Report' wizard showing 'HTML Report' selected, or a web browser/viewer displaying the generated Autopsy HTML report.

integrity.description: The agent used the Autopsy GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking through the Autopsy interface (wizards, context menus, tagging dialogs) rather than just running terminal scripts or directly modifying SQLite databases.

integrity.description: The agent used the application's tagging features to tag files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of Autopsy's native 'Tag and Comment' dialog boxes being actively used to apply the tags and comments.

integrity.description: The agent used Autopsy's built-in report generation feature.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 'Generate Report' dialog in Autopsy being used, rather than the agent manually typing an HTML file in a text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Case Name: 'Court_Prep_2024'. Case Number: 'INV-TAG-006'. Disk Image Path: '/home/ga/evidence/ntfs_undel.dd'. Expected Notable Item Comment: 'Deleted file - evidence of potential data destruction'. Expected Follow Up Comment: 'Active file - content review required for relevance'. The agent must use Autopsy to process the image using ONLY the 'File Type Identification' module, tag the files appropriately, and generate an HTML report.
